overview.description: AI-powered software for data-driven tree condition assessment and arborist workflows
overview.description_long: ARBO Technologies develops a suite of AI and sensor tools for arborists to assess the structural stability and health of non-forest trees. Their three products — Adbian (3D stability analysis software), TreeScanner (mobile data collection app), and TreeWatch (vehicle-mounted hardware sensor) — combine neural networks, LiDAR, 3D terrain modelling, and automated photo analysis to give arborists objective data on tree risk, replacing guesswork with evidence-based decisions. The company is based in Brno and has global ambitions, partnering with Safe Trees and Y Soft, and was incubated under the Czech Technologická inkubace programme.

history.origin_story: Founded in February 2021 in Brno, Czech Republic, combining deep arboristika field expertise with cutting-edge AI and sensor technology to improve tree risk assessment.

key_milestones.event: Adbian project supported by Technologická inkubace programme
Partnered with Safe Trees and Y Soft

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Core value is in physics-based tree stability simulation (Adbian), calibrated LiDAR/sensor hardware (TreeWatch), and domain-specific arboristika expertise — not a thin LLM wrapper. Foundation models cannot replicate vehicle-mounted sensor hardware or certified structural load analysis.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Real, proven demand from municipalities and utility operators needing tree risk management, but the total addressable market is niche and geographically fragmented. Revenue scale potential is limited without international expansion.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Very early-stage startup (founded 2021) with no known VC funding — only incubation programme support. Small team with unclear runway, making survival to scale dependent on early commercial traction.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Strong technical moat in hardware+AI tree diagnostics insulates against AGI commoditisation, but high funding risk and a niche market cap constrain long-term growth. Overall risk is medium — viable niche product, uncertain path to scale.

metadata.notes: Company is headquartered in Brno (not Prague). Included in Czech AI catalog. Website is in Czech; English version partially available. Products: Adbian (3D stability analysis software), TreeScanner (mobile app), TreeWatch (vehicle-mounted sensor device). Adbian supported by Czech Technologická inkubace programme.
